subject: Chemistry
title: Rust: The Slow Burn
description: A short lesson to help you apply Rust: The Slow Burn.

hook.text: Rust is iron slowly burning. The same reaction as fire—just a billion times slower.

buildup.text: Iron reacts with oxygen and water to form iron oxide—a flaky, reddish-brown coating we call rust.

discovery.text: Both rusting and combustion are oxidation. Iron loses electrons to oxygen in both. Speed is the only difference.

twist.text: Rust expands to six times the volume of the original iron, cracking concrete and collapsing bridges.

climax.text: Corrosion costs the world $2.5 trillion per year. Humanity's battle against rust never ends.

punchline.text: Iron burning in slow motion. Rust destroys more than fire.

quiz.question: How is rust chemically similar to fire?
options: Both are oxidation reactions where a material reacts with oxygen
Both require extreme heat to start
